properties.title: Julius Caesar invades Britain the first time Kent 55BCE
properties.content: in 55 BCE Julius Ceasar invades Britain as part of his Gallic Wars.
properties.attribution: Classical Numismatic Group, Inc. http://www.cngcoins.com, CC BY-SA 2.5 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.5>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Julius Caesar invades Britain again Middlesex 54 BCE
properties.content: Julius Ceasar invades England a 2nd time in 54BCE claiming they were "supporting his enemies"
properties.attribution: Museum of antiquities, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Aulus Plautius of Rome Invades britain for the third time southeast england 43 AD
properties.content: in 43AD Aulus Plautius invades England fully taking control over it this time.
properties.attribution: my work, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Ostorious lures Caratacus into a set piece battle and defeats him
properties.content: Roman Governor Publius Ostorius Scapula lures rebel leader of the Silures Caratacus into a set-piece battle and defeats him and his rebelion in 51AD
properties.attribution: Ad Meskens, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Caratacus flees North England 50AD
properties.content: After losing Caratacus flees the battle and heads to the brigantes who turn him in to the romans
properties.attribution: Andrew Birrell (fl. 1782–1809), after Henry Fuseli (1741–1825), Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Ostorius is rewarded Rome 50AD
properties.content: Ostorius Scapula was awarded Triumphal Ornaments for defeating him by Emperor Claudius
properties.attribution: British Museum, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Caratacus persuades Emperor to spare his life Rome 50AD
properties.content: After his defeat Caratacus is taken to rome and gives a speech infront of the Emporer who spares his life.
properties.attribution: See page for author, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Venutius replaces Caratacus North England 51AD
properties.content: After Caratacus's defeat Venutius becomes leader of the Silurian british resistance.
properties.attribution: The original uploader was StephenDawson at English Wikipedia., CC BY-SA 2.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Nero extends Roman Empire to Lindum Lincoln England 54AD
properties.content: Nero becomes emperor of Rome and exands the empire to london in 54AD
properties.attribution: Glyptothek, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Gaius Suetonius Paulinus becomes govenor of Britain Britain 58AD
properties.content: Nero appoints Gaius Suetonius Paulinus (the conqueror of Mauretania) to Governor of Britain in 58AD
properties.attribution: Ad Meskens, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Suetonius attacks the druids Anglesay England 61AD
properties.content: in 60AD Suetonius attacks Mona (Anglesay) to settle accounts with the druids massacaring them and destroying their sacred groves
properties.attribution: Sterim64, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Boudica Revolts Southeast Britain 61AD
properties.content: While Suetonius is campaigning against the druids the southeast of England revolted against the Roman Empire under the leadership of Boudica
properties.attribution: John Opie, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Boudica destroys Camulodunum Colchester England 61AD
properties.content: Boudica and her tribe joined forces with the Trinovantes to revolt against what they believed to be the oppression of the roman empire and sacked and destroyed the roman colony at Camulodunum
properties.attribution: Dr Neil Clifton / Colchester Castle

properties.title: Romans abandon Londinium London England 61AD
properties.content: The Rebels Ride towards their next target Londinium as does Suetonius. Suetonius decides Lodinium could not be defended and abandons it.
properties.attribution: Fremantleboy, Drallim (translation), CC BY 2.5 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.5>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Boudica sacks and destroys Londinium London England 61AD
properties.content: Boudica and her group of rebels sack and Pillage Londinium now known as London.
properties.attribution: Rafesmar, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Boudica sacks and Destroys Verulamium St Albans England 61AD
properties.content: After London Boudica takes her group of rebels to Verulamium now known as St Albans and destroys it as well killing between 70-80 thousand people across three cities
properties.attribution: Przemysław Sakrajda, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Paulinus defeats Boudica at the Battle of Watling Flintshire Wales 61AD
properties.content: In 61AD Paulinus finally has his stand off with Boudica at the battle of Watling Street and defeats her and her army.
properties.attribution: Amitchell125, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Boudica dies Flintshire Wales 61AD
properties.content: Boudica dies of unknown causes shortly after her defeat it is not known weather she killed herself or died of an illness of some sort
properties.attribution: Charles Hamilton Smith, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Venutius takes control of the north of Britain North Britain 69AD
properties.content: in 69AD Venutius of the Brigantes defeats the Romans in Cartimandua and gains control over the north of the country.
properties.attribution: England_Celtic_tribes_-_North_and_Midlands.png: self-createdderivative work: Jpb1301, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Vespian appoints new govenors England 69AD
properties.content: Quintus Pettilias Cerialis and Sextus Julius Frontinus are appointed Governor of England By Vespian
properties.attribution: Carole Raddato from FRANKFURT, Germany, CC BY-SA 2.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Frontinus extends Roman Rule to all of South Wales South Wales 69AD
properties.content: Newly appointed governor Sextus Julius Frontinus extended Roman Rule to all of South Wales in 69AD.
properties.attribution: Dank · Jay, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Agricola conquers the Ordovices Wales and England 78AD
properties.content: Governor Julius Agricola Expanded Roman control of Britain even more by conquering the Ordovices tribe in 78AD
properties.attribution: author of source image, plus my additions (myself), CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Agricola defeats Caledonians North-East Scotland 84AD
properties.content: Governor Julius Agricola Expands Roman Control over Britain even more by conquering the Caledonians at the battle of Mons Graupis in 84AD
properties.attribution: (myself), CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Rome abandons lands north of Cheviot Hills Anglo-Scottish Border 87AD
properties.content: In 87AD the decision was made for Rome to Abandon most of their lands North of the Cheviot Hills to allow for troops to be moved to other frontiers. This decision upset Governor Agricola.
properties.attribution: Stuart Meek, CC BY-SA 2.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Hadrian orders Hadrians wall to be built Northumberland England 120AD
properties.content: in 120AD Hadrian orders an extensive wall be built to keep invaders from the north out.
properties.attribution: quisnovus from Gloucester, England, CC BY 2.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Antoninus Pius extends hadrianic border north Scotland 142AD
properties.content: in 142 AD Antoninus Pius extended the hardrianic border north through military conquest.
properties.attribution: Hadrians_Wall_map.png: Created by NormanEinstein, September 20, 2005derivative work: Talifero, CC BY-SA 3.0 <http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Brigantes revolt North West England 155-157AD
properties.content: From 155-157AD the Brigantes revolted causing the Roman troops to retreat south before recapturing the wall in 157AD
properties.attribution: Quijav, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Antiones wall is abandoned Scotland 164AD
properties.content: in 164AD Rome decides Antiones wall is not worth defending any longer an abandon it.
properties.attribution: Jim Knowles, CC BY 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Picts breach Hadrians wall Brampton England 180AD
properties.content: in 180AD Picts breach Hadrians wall and kill the commanding officer/governor
properties.attribution: Catfish Jim and the soapdish at English Wikipedia, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Ulpius Marcellus defeats the Picts Brampton England 184AD
properties.content: in 184AD Newly appointed governor Ulpius Marcellus defeats the Picts only to face Mutiny from his own troops shortly after
properties.attribution: myself, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Northern England Erupts into anarchy North England 207AD
properties.content: in 207 AD during Albinus's abscence from Britain Northern England faces anarchy with many tribes warring against each other and the romans.
properties.attribution: Equestenebrarum, CC BY 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Severus unsuccesfully invades Caledonia Scotland 209AD
properties.content: in 209AD Severus launches a campaign into Caledonia Modern day Scotland in an attempt to tame the tribes of the north but as a result of the Picts and other tribes guerrilla strategies he was unsuccesful.
properties.attribution: Jrockley, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Severus divides Britain York England 210AD
properties.content: in 210AD Severus attempts to end the constant civil wars throughout Britain by dividing Britain into Britannia superior and Britannia Inferior.
properties.attribution: Joan Blaeu, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Gallic Empire is formed and Britain Joins Britain 259AD
properties.content: in 259AD as a result of economic hardship Postumus Rebels against Gallienus and a short lived Gallic Empire is formed inlcuding Britain.
properties.attribution: Original - ArdadN ; edit - Wario2., Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Aurelian reunites the empire Britain 274AD
properties.content: in 274AD After 15 years of seperation Aurelian Reunites the Empire bringing Britain and Rome together once again.
properties.attribution: Giovanni Dall'Orto., Attribution, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Britannic Empire is formed Britain 286AD
properties.content: The Carausian revolt in 286AD resulted in the short lived formation of a Britannic Empire.
properties.attribution: Shgasfhgu, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Britannic Empire falls Britain 296AD
properties.content: in 296AD the Britannic Empire falls and Britain is under the control of Rome once again.
properties.attribution: Criscombat, CC0, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Britain gets divided into four provinces Britain 312AD
properties.content: Due to reform that was carried out by 312AD Britain is divided into 4 diocese: Maxima Caesariensis, Flavia Caesariensis, Britannia Prima, and Britannia Secunda.
properties.attribution: Lotroo, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Constantine campaigns against the Picts Scotland 306AD
properties.content: in 306AD Constantine arrived in britain in poor health with hopes of conquering the north and succeded.
properties.attribution: Merulana, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Roman Britain is attacked by Numerous groups Britain 367AD
properties.content: in 367AD Saxons, Picts, Scoti, and Attacotti all launch an attack on Hadrians Wall known as "The Great Conspiracy" and overwhelm the entire western and northern regions of Britannia
properties.attribution: This work is in the public domain in its country of origin and other countries and areas where the copyright term is the author's life plus 70 years or fewer.

properties.title: Theodosius ends the invasion Britain 368AD
properties.content: in 368AD the Rebelion is crushed by Theodosius the elder through various military and civil reforms.
properties.attribution: my work, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

properties.title: Rome slowly loses its influence in Britain Britain 410AD
properties.content: starting in 400AD Rome slowly loses their influence over Britain.
properties.attribution: my work, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons
